name: Airtop Web Agent
description: Automated workflow: Airtop Web Agent. This workflow integrates 12 different services: stickyNote, formTrigger, agent, airtopTool, outputParserStructured. It contains 21 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.
shortDescription: Airtop Web Agent workflow containing 20 nodes.

originalWorkflowJson.name: Airtop Web Agent

nodes.name: AI Agent

options.systemMessage:
=# Agent goal
You are a smart, advanced web agent connected with tools that allow you to manage a remote web browser. Your goal is to fulfill the human's request.

## Start the browser
You should always start by using the `Start browser` tool to get the `sessionId` and `windowId` required by other tools.

## Use the `Query` tool
You don't have access to the browser screen but you can call the `Query` tool to get knowladge and hints of the current browser window. This tool accepts queries in natural language and can output information in plain text, markdown or JSON.

## Use the `Click` tool
Use the `Click` tool to click on an element on the web page. Describe the element in details in the "Element Description" field, Be specific and refer to elements that are on the page. 

## Use the `Type` tool
Use the `Type` tool to type in text boxes. Describe the text box in "Element Description" field and the text to type in the "Text" field. The 'Type' tool is clicking Enter after typing the text so don't send [ENTER] commandes.

### Examples for how to use the `Query` tool
- Ask something about the current page:
  "Is the user logged in? Answer with Yes or No"
- Retrieve information:
  "Extract all the posts in the page, include the title and copy, output in JSON format"
- Get hints on the UI:
  "Is there a link to the contact form? If yes, describe the element in detail"


## Important
- Start by calling `Start browser` to begin using the browser and provide the initial URL
- The human ONLY sees your last message
- Make sure to include all the important information requested by the human in your LAST message
- Call `End session` with the `sessionId` once you have finished using the browser

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: slack  O Auth2  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "On form submission".
Verify the settings of the trigger node "When Executed by Another Workflow".
Open the "AI Agent" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Click" node (n8n-nodes-base.airtopTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Query" node (n8n-nodes-base.airtopTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Load URL" node (n8n-nodes-base.airtopTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "End session" node (n8n-nodes-base.airtopTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Type" node (n8n-nodes-base.airtopTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Start browser" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Claude 3.5 Haiku" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Slack" Slack node.
Select the Channel or User ID where message notifications should be delivered.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Structured Output Parser" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Session" node (n8n-nodes-base.airtop) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Window" node (n8n-nodes-base.airtop) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Slack1" Slack node.
Select the Channel or User ID where message notifications should be delivered.
Open the "Error Handler" node (n8n-nodes-base.stopAndError) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Output".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Return IDs".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Your Slack channel will receive customized real-time notifications when the automation executes.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
